components.name: sodium lactate
components.evidence: S. oneidensis MR-1 and its high c-di-GMP transconjugant for enhanced biofilm formation ... were grown in a modified MR-1 medium (Cao et al., 2011) using 20 mM sodium lactate. ([3-13C]sodium lactate used for 13C-MFA.)

components.name: PIPES (piperazine-N,N'-bis(2-ethanesulfonic acid))
components.source: cited:Pinchuk 2010 (M1 medium; Fredrickson-lineage Shewanella MR-1 defined medium)
components.evidence: piperazine-N,N'-bis(2-ethanesulfonic acid (30 mM and 3 mM for batch and chemostat cultivation, respectively)

components.evidence: 4.35 mM NaH2PO4•H2O

components.evidence: 1 µM Na2SeO4

components.name: nitrilotriacetic acid
components.source: stock:Wolfe's mineral solution (DSMZ MediaDive / ATCC trace mineral supplement, added 10 mL/L)
components.evidence: Nitrilotriacetic acid 1.50 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution). Medium supplemented with '10 ml ... of ... 10x mineral solution.'

components.name: magnesium sulfate (MgSO4·7H2O)
components.evidence: MgSO4 3.00 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution). In-medium value matches target 0.12171 mM exactly (3.0 g/L / 100 / 246.5).

components.name: manganese sulfate (MnSO4·H2O)
components.evidence: MnSO4 0.50 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution). Matches target 0.02958 mM.

components.name: ferrous sulfate (FeSO4·7H2O)
components.evidence: FeSO4 0.10 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution). Matches target 0.0036 mM.

components.name: cobalt chloride (CoCl2·6H2O)
components.evidence: CoCl2 0.10 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution). Matches target 0.0042 mM.

components.name: zinc chloride (ZnCl2)
components.concentration: 1.3 mg/L (Wolfe's/ATCC trace mineral 0.13 g/L stock @ 10 mL/L)
components.source: stock:Wolfe's mineral solution (ATCC trace mineral supplement variant)
components.evidence: Wolfe's mineral solution contains a zinc salt (DSMZ lists ZnSO4 0.10 g/L; the ATCC 'trace mineral supplement' variant uses ZnCl2 0.13 g/L). Target 0.00954 mM = 0.13 g/L / 100 / 136.3, confirming the ZnCl2 variant.

components.name: copper sulfate (CuSO4·5H2O)
components.evidence: CuSO4 0.01 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution). Matches target 0.0004 mM.

components.name: aluminum potassium sulfate (AlK(SO4)2·12H2O)
components.evidence: AlK(SO4)2 0.01 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution).

components.name: boric acid (H3BO3)
components.evidence: H3BO3 0.01 g ... per 1000 mL (Wolfe's Mineral Solution).

components.name: sodium molybdate (Na2MoO4·2H2O)
components.concentration: 0.25 mg/L (Wolfe's/ATCC trace mineral 0.025 g/L stock @ 10 mL/L)
components.source: stock:Wolfe's mineral solution (ATCC trace mineral supplement variant)
components.evidence: Wolfe's mineral solution contains Na2MoO4 (DSMZ 0.01 g/L; ATCC variant 0.025 g/L). Target 0.00103 mM = 0.025 g/L / 100 / 242, confirming the ATCC variant.

components.name: nickel chloride (NiCl2·6H2O)
components.concentration: 0.24 mg/L (ATCC trace mineral 0.024 g/L stock @ 10 mL/L)
components.source: stock:Wolfe's mineral solution (ATCC trace mineral supplement variant)
components.evidence: NiCl2·6H2O is present in the ATCC 'trace mineral supplement' (Wolfe's) variant at 0.024 g/L (the base DSMZ 2680 list omits Ni). Target 0.00101 mM = 0.024 g/L / 100 / 237.7, confirming the ATCC variant.

components.source: cited:Nakagawa 2015 amino-acid solution (as reprinted verbatim in AEM 2023, PMC10370299)
components.evidence: 10 mL L-1 each of amino acid solution (containing 2 g L-1 each of l-glutamic acid, l-arginine, and dl-serine)

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: amino acid solution (containing 2 g L-1 each of l-glutamic acid, l-arginine, and dl-serine)

components.name: DL-serine
components.evidence: amino acid solution (containing 2 g L-1 each of l-glutamic acid, l-arginine, and dl-serine)

components.name: O2 (aerobic)
components.evidence: S. oneidensis MR-1 grown aerobically (organism is a facultative anaerobe; this test grown under the aerobic MR-1/M1 defined-medium regime).

media.base_medium: Fredrickson/PNNL-lineage Shewanella oneidensis MR-1 defined medium (a.k.a. 'MR-1 medium' / 'M1 medium'): PIPES-buffered, lactate as electron donor, supplemented with Wolfe's mineral solution, Wolfe's vitamin solution and an amino-acid stock; modified here to 20 mM sodium lactate.

sources_chased.what: PMC6255981 methods (statement of the medium name + lactate)
added: medium name 'modified MR-1 medium (Cao et al., 2011)'

sources_chased.what: Cao et al. 2011 (Environ Microbiol 13:1018-1031, doi 10.1111/j.1462-2920.2010.02407.x) full recipe
sources_chased.how: Wiley (sfamjournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com) returned 403; only Cao PhD-thesis abstract (OSTI 1105046) was retrievable and it does not reprint the recipe

sources_chased.what: Base MR-1/M1 defined-medium recipe (Marshall 2006 -> Beliaev 2005 -> Myers & Nealson 1988 chain)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch PMC1526764 (Marshall 2006) and PMC1251602 (Beliaev 2005); these cite the base recipe but do not reprint stocks
added: confirmed lineage uses PIPES-buffered lactate defined medium with Wolfe's mineral+vitamin stocks

sources_chased.what: Full M1 base-salt recipe with exact concentrations
added: 10 mL/L each Wolfe's vitamin + mineral solution

sources_chased.how: WebFetch DSMZ MediaDive solution 2680; concentrations cross-validated against target values which match the ATCC 'trace mineral supplement' variant to 3 sig figs
WebFetch AEM 2023 PMC10370299 (reprints the Shewanella MR-1 amino-acid solution verbatim, cited to Nakagawa 2015)

missing_before: magnesium chloride (3 mM MgCl2, bulk Mg)
Wolfe's vitamin solution: biotin, folic acid, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboflavin, nicotinic acid, Ca-pantothenate, B12, PABA, lipoic acid
amino acid solution: L-glutamic acid, L-arginine, DL-serine
media.notes: The exact Cao et al. 2011 primary text is paywalled (Wiley 403; ACS companion es200095j also inaccessible), so the recipe was reconstructed from the well-documented Fredrickson/PNNL Shewanella MR-1 'M1' defined-medium lineage (Marshall 2006 -> Beliaev 2005; full salts from Pinchuk 2010). Reconstruction is strongly validated: the target's own bulk-salt and trace-metal concentrations match this recipe (Wolfe's mineral solution @ 10 mL/L) to 3 significant figures (MgSO4 0.12171 mM, MnSO4 0.02958, FeSO4 0.0036, ZnCl2 0.00954, Na2MoO4 0.00103, NiCl2 0.00101, CoCl2 0.0042, CuSO4 0.0004, CaCl2 6.8 uM, KCl 1.34 mM, NH4Cl 28 mM). The two previously 'uncovered' items (PIPES, nitrilotriacetic acid, boric acid) plus the vitamin and amino-acid stocks are now fully expanded. MR-1 is a facultative anaerobe; oxygen kept 'aerobic' per the target and paper. The bulk NaCl 30 mM and MgCl2 3 mM are standard M1 components (confidence medium: could differ slightly in Cao's exact 'modified' formulation, which could not be retrieved).
